Run the local file-backed editorial dashboard

Usage:
  laurel dashboard [--config <path>] [--dev] [--no-build] [--port <n>] [--host <host>] [--open] [--json]

Options:
  -c, --config <path> Config path(s); repeat or comma-separate to deep-merge in order
  --dev               Run the dashboard with Bun's fullstack dev server (HMR for src/cli/dashboard/web/**; no pre-built bundle required)
  --no-build          Skip the prod-from-source auto-build and serve the embedded/pre-built bundle as-is (fast restart; no effect on --dev or the published CLI)
  -p, --port <n>      Port to listen on (0..65535 integer; defaults to 4322; pass 0 to let the kernel pick a free port)
  --host <host>       Hostname to bind to (defaults to 127.0.0.1 for local-only file editing; pass 0.0.0.0 to expose on the LAN)
  --open              Open the dashboard in the default browser after the server starts
  -j, --json          Switch logger output (lifecycle events) to one JSON object per line for CI / log forwarders
  -h, --help          Show help for this command

Environment variables:
  Every flag has an env var fallback (CLI flag > env var > .laurelrc > config > default).
  Naming: LAUREL_<COMMAND>_<FLAG> (uppercased, dashes become underscores).
  Example: --config → LAUREL_DASHBOARD_CONFIG

Repeated flags:
  Scalar string flags use the last value.
  List-style string flags accumulate as comma-separated values in argv order.
  Boolean flags use the last positive or negated spelling.

Examples:
  laurel dashboard                             # http://127.0.0.1:4322 local dashboard
  laurel dashboard --open                      # launch the browser after startup
  laurel dashboard --port 0                    # pick a free port for smoke tests
  laurel dashboard --host 0.0.0.0              # expose on the LAN
  laurel dashboard --dev                       # frontend HMR; bundles TSX/CSS on demand
  laurel dashboard --no-build                  # serve the embedded bundle without rebuilding
